    <p>{`The documentation lives in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` folder.`}</p>
    <p>{`We build the `}<a parentName="p" {...{

    <p>{`The build copies the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` folder to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages/gatsby-docs`}</inlineCode>{`, makes some minor alternations to make it work with the Carbon theme, and deploys it. Specifically it will convert the level 1 headings into “title” attributes, and it will remove the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`[<< Back to previous page]`}</inlineCode>{` links.`}</p>
    <p>{`Doing this allows us to keep our documentation files in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`docs`}</inlineCode>{` folder in case we ever need to switch back to using standard Github pages (for example if there is an error in the Gatsby deploy.)`}</p>
    <p>{`Note that the Gatsby navigation sidebar is hand-crafted, so if you add a new page or move or rename an existing page you will have to update `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`packages/gatsby-docs/src/data/nav-items.yaml`}</inlineCode>{` to match.`}</p>
